label: PASS
rationale: With KeepGlobalRangeKey=True and the Global Range not both read-locked and write-locked (read locked, write unlocked), RevertSP is required to preserve the Global Range key/data and may complete successfully.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Global Range is write-unlocked, so RevertSP with KeepGlobalRangeKey=True should comply and complete successfully; returning FAIL is inconsistent with the rule.

label: PASS
rationale: With KeepGlobalRangeKey=True and the Global Range read-unlocked (though write-locked), the specification says the TPer shall comply with keeping the Global Range data/key, so SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Global Range is read-unlocked, so RevertSP with KeepGlobalRangeKey=True should succeed; FAIL is only required when the range is both read-locked and write-locked.

label: PASS
rationale: The Global Range is both read-locked and write-locked, and KeepGlobalRangeKey=True was supplied; the specification requires RevertSP to fail with FAIL and not change lifecycle state.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because the Global Range is both read-locked and write-locked, RevertSP with KeepGlobalRangeKey=True must fail with FAIL and leave the SP lifecycle unchanged; SUCCESS is noncompliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The prior RevertSP with KeepGlobalRangeKey=True succeeded while the Global Range was write-unlocked, so the TPer must keep the Global Range user data and media key; reading back the written pattern is consistent.

label: FAIL
rationale: The preceding RevertSP omitted KeepGlobalRangeKey, so a successful Locking SP RevertSP causes user data removal and media key eradication; preserving and reading Pattern A5 is noncompliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After successful RevertSP of a manufactured Locking SP to its Original Factory State, the Locking SP returns to Manufactured-Inactive, and sessions cannot be opened to SPs in that state; StartSession SUCCESS is noncompliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: A successful RevertSP without KeepGlobalRangeKey returns the Locking SP to Manufactured-Inactive and aborts the session; subsequent Locking SP management via Set should not succeed because sessions cannot be opened and locking management is disabled in Manufactured-Inactive.

label: PASS
rationale: The previous RevertSP with KeepGlobalRangeKey=True failed because the Global Range was both read-locked and write-locked, so the SP did not change lifecycle state. After EndSession, opening a new session to the still-Manufactured Locking SP is consistent.
